taxonomy.family_description: Cashew/Mango family

climate.temp_optimum: 24-30°C during vegetative growth; cool dry period needed for flowering
climate.rainfall: 750-2500mm; dry period during flowering and fruiting essential

irrigation.method: Drip irrigation preferred for bearing orchards

harvesting.method: Hand-pick with harvesting poles; 2cm stalk retained to prevent sap burn

symptoms.early: Dark brown to black spots on young leaves, flowers, and immature fruits; 'blossom blight' killing flower clusters
symptoms.late: Sunken, dark brown lesions on mature fruits that expand rapidly post-harvest; fruit rot; complete flower destruction

treatment.organic: Copper oxychloride 3g/L, neem oil + copper, hot water dip 52°C for 5 minutes (post-harvest)
treatment.chemical: Carbendazim 0.1% + Mancozeb 0.25% spray, Azoxystrobin + Difenoconazole, Propiconazole 0.1%
treatment.preventive: Prune dead wood, copper spray before flowering, proper post-harvest handling (hot water), waxing, cold chain

symptoms.early: Vegetative malformation: bunchy top appearance with compacted proliferation of shoots ('mango witches' broom')
symptoms.late: Floral malformation: panicle conversion to compact, sterile, green vegetative structure; no fruits formed

treatment.organic: Remove and burn malformed parts; control mites with acaricides
treatment.chemical: NAA (Naphthaleneacetic acid) 200ppm spray + Carbendazim (0.1%) at panicle emergence
treatment.preventive: Use disease-free planting material, certified nursery plants, deinfest cutting tools, control mite vectors

symptoms.early: White powdery coating on young leaves, flower clusters, and young fruits
symptoms.late: Complete whitening of panicles, flower drop, young fruit drop, malformed surviving fruits

treatment.organic: Wettable sulfur 3g/L at bud break; diluted milk spray (1:5); potassium bicarbonate
treatment.chemical: Triadimefon 25% WP (1g/L), Hexaconazole 5% EC (2ml/L), Propiconazole 25% EC (1ml/L), Myclobutanil
treatment.preventive: First spray at bud emergence, second at fruit set; avoid overhead irrigation; morning sprays

pests.damage_type: Oviposition and larval feeding inside fruit

control.organic: Male attractant traps (Methyl eugenol + malathion or GF-120); fruit bagging with paper or plastic bags; Protein bait sprays; pick and destroy infested fallen fruit
control.chemical: Protein bait spray (GF-120 bait + Spinosad); Cover sprays Malathion 50% EC (2ml/L), Dimethoate (2ml/L); only during non-harvest period
control.biological: Fopius arisanus (egg-larval parasitoid), Diachasmimorpha longicaudata, Fopius vandenboschi, mass rearing and release of sterile males (SIT)

pests.scientific_name: Phenacoccus solenopsis (Cotton Mealybug); Planococcus citri (Citrus Mealybug)

control.organic: Neem oil + soap spray; rubbing alcohol on cotton swabs; strong water spray; remove waxy coating first; Cryptolaemus montrouzieri release
control.chemical: Imidacloprid soil drench (2ml/L), Buprofezin 25% WP (1g/L), Chlorpyrifos 20% EC (2.5ml/L), Acetamiprid + Chlorpyrifos
control.biological: Cryptolaemus montrouzieri (mealybug destroyer beetle), Leptomastix dactylopii (parasitoid wasp), Anagyrus kamali, Acerophagus papayae

pests.scientific_name: Diaspidiotus perniciosus (San Jose Scale); Saissetia oleae (Olive Scale)

control.organic: Dormant oil spray (petroleum oil 3-5%) at crawler stage; white summer oil (1-2%); brush off with stiff brush
control.chemical: Buprofezin (at crawler stage), Chlorpyrifos + oil, Spirotetramat systemic; dormant oil during winter
control.biological: Encarsia perniciosi (parasitoid of San Jose scale), Comperiella bifasciata, Aphytis melinus (red scale parasitoid)

control.organic: Collect and destroy fallen infested fruits; tree hygiene — remove old bark; hot water seed treatment for nursery use (52°C for 15 min)
control.chemical: Chlorpyrifos 20% EC (2ml/L) spray when adults are active (Feb-March); trunk banding with insecticide strips

taxonomy.family_description: Banana family

climate.rainfall: 2000-2500mm; well distributed throughout year; critical to avoid dry spells

harvesting.method: Whole bunch harvested at once by cutting bunch stalk

symptoms.early: Tiny pale yellow flecks on lower leaf surface that elongate into brown streaks
symptoms.late: Brown-black elongated lesions with yellow halo on upper leaf surface; premature defoliation; green fruit ripening prematurely

treatment.organic: Copper oxychloride (2-3g/L) sprays, oil-based copper formulations; remove infected leaves
treatment.chemical: Systemic fungicides on rotation: Propiconazole, Tebuconazole, Fenbuconazole, Chlorothalonil; resistance management essential
treatment.preventive: Resistant varieties, remove lower infected leaves (deleafing), reduce humidity with good drainage and canopy management

symptoms.early: Yellowing of older outer leaves, leaf collapse, starting from leaf margins; brown streaking in pseudostem when cut
symptoms.late: Complete plant collapse, pseudostem brown-red internal discoloration from base upward, no fruit production

treatment.organic: No cure. Soil solarization, biological soil amendments (Trichoderma, compost)
treatment.biological: Trichoderma koningii, T. harzianum as suppressants
treatment.preventive: Strict quarantine, plant resistant varieties (GCTCV-218 for TR4), clean tools with bleach, avoid infected soil movement

pests.damage_type: Oviposition and larval feeding inside fruit

control.organic: Male attractant traps (Methyl eugenol + malathion or GF-120); fruit bagging with paper or plastic bags; Protein bait sprays; pick and destroy infested fallen fruit
control.chemical: Protein bait spray (GF-120 bait + Spinosad); Cover sprays Malathion 50% EC (2ml/L), Dimethoate (2ml/L); only during non-harvest period
control.biological: Fopius arisanus (egg-larval parasitoid), Diachasmimorpha longicaudata, Fopius vandenboschi, mass rearing and release of sterile males (SIT)

control.biological: Fopius arisanus (egg-larval parasitoid), Diachasmimorpha longicaudata, Fopius vandenboschi, mass rearing and release of sterile males (SIT)

Rose family; includes apples, pears, cherries, plums, strawberries

symptoms.early: Small, soft, round galls develop at crown (soil line) or on roots
symptoms.late: Galls enlarge to large rough, brown, corky tumors; reduced plant vigor, stunting, increased susceptibility to other diseases

treatment.organic: Cut out galls; treat with Agrobacterium radiobacter strain K-84 (biocontrol) at planting
treatment.chemical: No effective post-infection treatment; copper bactericides preventively
treatment.biological: Agrobacterium radiobacter strain K-84 / K-1026 biocontrol (prevents infection)
treatment.preventive: Avoid wounding, use K-84 dip on roots before planting, plant only certified gall-free material

control.biological: Encarsia perniciosi (parasitoid of San Jose scale), Comperiella bifasciata, Aphytis melinus (red scale parasitoid)

symptoms.early: Water-soaked, brown lesions on petals, leaves, and stems; soft rot
symptoms.late: Characteristic gray fluffy fungal sporulation covering lesions; blight of flowers, stem cankers, fruit rot ("ghost spot" on tomato)
symptoms.affected_parts: Flowers, Leaves, Stems, Fruit, Storage organs

treatment.organic: Remove infected tissue, improve air circulation, reduce humidity; Bacillus subtilis, Trichoderma spray
treatment.chemical: Iprodione (1.5g/L), Pyrimethanil (1ml/L), Fenhexamid (1g/L), Cyprodinil (0.5g/L). Rotate fungicides for resistance management.
treatment.biological: Bacillus subtilis QST713 (Serenade), Trichoderma asperellum, Clonostachys rosea (Prestop)
treatment.preventive: Improve air circulation, avoid wounding, remove dead tissue, avoid waterlogging, maintain low humidity

symptoms.late: Characteristic gray fluffy fungal sporulation covering lesions; blight of flowers, stem cankers, fruit rot ("ghost spot" on tomato)

treatment.biological: Bacillus subtilis QST713 (Serenade), Trichoderma asperellum, Clonostachys rosea (Prestop)
treatment.preventive: Improve air circulation, avoid wounding, remove dead tissue, avoid waterlogging, maintain low humidity

pests.scientific_name: Amrasca biguttula biguttula (Cotton/Okra Leafhopper); Empoasca spp.

control.organic: Neem seed kernel extract 5%, neem oil; yellow sticky traps; Beauveria bassiana spray
control.chemical: Imidacloprid 17.8% SL (0.3ml/L), Lambda-cyhalothrin 5% EC (1ml/L), Thiamethoxam, Dimethoate 30% EC (2ml/L)
control.biological: Anagrus atomus (egg parasitoid in grapes), Gonatocerus ashmeadi, Stethorus ladybugs
